definition: The current dollar regime sits in a transitional/unstable zone where the status quo is unsustainable over 12-24 months, but direction of resolution is uncertain. US growth differentials narrowing from +2pp (2023) toward +0.5-1.0pp (2026). DXY shows pattern of lower highs (114 to 108 to 107) since September 2022. Cross-asset inconsistency exists: rates pricing 50-75bp cuts vs DXY resilience at 103-106.
evidence: US growth differentials narrowing from +2pp (2023) toward +0.5-1.0pp (2026)
Rates pricing 50-75bp cuts while DXY resilient at 103-106
Fiscal dollar smile counter: fiscal expansion structurally supports dollar in middle zone
Muddled middle can persist: 2018-2019 DXY traded 95-99 for nearly two years
open_questions: Whether the muddled middle can persist beyond 24 months given fiscal dynamics
Whether AI capex structural capital inflows represent a structural break in dollar cycles
Whether fiscal expansion creates an asymmetric smirk that prevents dollar weakness
